paper_claim: On the 203-image benchmark, GROVE outperformed the best tested single-pass baseline in hazard identification.
boundary: Applies to the tested open-weight systems and benchmark.

paper_claim: GROVE's grounding advantage is strongest under strict localization and joint box-and-label criteria, rather than every IoG threshold.
boundary: Raw IoG ranking changes because broad boxes can score well.

paper_claim: Performance is strongest on adequately supported categories and should be interpreted cautiously for sparse categories.
evidence: F1 >= 0.75 for Fall Hazard, Electrocution Hazard, PPE Violation, Housekeeping/Storage; Struck-by support=3 and Caught-in/Between support=10.
boundary: Sparse-category estimates have wide or degenerate intervals.

paper_claim: Primary GroundingDINO explains most cached grounding coverage, while fallback grounding mainly recovers otherwise ungrounded hazards.
evidence: Trace control GT coverage 0.7530; GroundingDINO-only 0.7328; no-fallback no-box rate 0.1507.

paper_claim: Path 2 verification and deterministic reconciliation provide small incremental improvements in the matched trace control.
evidence: Trace control F1 0.5090; no Path 2 0.5045; union reconciliation 0.5062.
boundary: Descriptive trace-level deltas; not compared with the archived run.

paper_claim: The full system produces fewer false-positive no-hazard images than the tested single-pass baselines.
boundary: Do not attribute the difference causally to compliance rules.

paper_claim: Reporting both seven-category and six-category results improves transparency about the heterogeneous Other category.
boundary: Evaluation-only; it does not measure prompt-level redistribution.
